sanskrit: श्रीभगवानुवाच। इमं विवस्वते योगं प्रोक्तवानहमव्ययम्। विवस्वान् मनवे प्राह मनुरिक्ष्वाकवेऽब्रवीत्॥
transliteration: śrī-bhagavān uvāca / imaṃ vivasvate yogaṃ proktavān aham avyayam / vivasvān manave prāha manur ikṣvākave 'bravīt
literal_meaning: The Blessed Lord said: 'This imperishable yoga I proclaimed to Vivasvān; Vivasvān told it to Manu, and Manu declared it to Ikṣvāku.'
contextual_meaning: Krishna reframes the dialogue from personal advice to timeless transmission, anchoring Arjuna’s crisis in an unbroken lineage. By invoking solar and royal archetypes, he signals that the forthcoming teaching is not novel therapy but perennial psychology—an ancestral immune response to recurring human despair.
psychological_state: Arjuna shifts from acute doubt to anticipatory receptivity; the egoic ‘I am stuck’ is replaced by the trans-personal ‘I am part of a continuum.’
trigger_scenario: A burned-out leader is told that the mindfulness manual she craves was once taught to her great-grandfather by a NASA astronaut, instantly shrinking her panic to a chapter in a longer story.
anchor_text: This wisdom is older than the sun, passed from king to king—listen.
